lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Hardware AES encryption instructions (Intel AES-NI / AMD equivalent) are active and standard in modern CPUs. AES-NI introduced with Intel Westmere (2010); AMD Bulldozer (2011). Extended with VAES (vectorized) in Intel Ice Lake, AMD Zen 3. Standard in all modern x86 processors.', 'source': 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_x86_cryptographic_instructions'}
notes: New hardware instructions in Westmere for encryption; reduce overhead for full disk encryption, compression, AV, VPN. Enables BitLocker adoption as business norm.
Expected to become business norm for full disk encryption enabled by hardware AES instructions.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Intel AMT (Active Management Technology) remains active in Intel vPro platform as of 2024. Individual features have been deprecated over time (SOAP interface, EAC, etc.) but the core AMT out-of-band management technology ships in current Intel vPro business laptops and desktops.', 'source': 'https://software.intel.com/sites/manageability/AMT_Implementation_and_Reference_Guide/WordDocuments/deprecatedanddeletedfeatures.htm'}
notes: vPro subset enabling out-of-band remote diagnosis, repair, restart of notebooks even when OS is down.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'VPN technology remains active. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
